channel.title: MyHistamate — Blog
channel.description: Honest, practical writing about life with histamine intolerance (HIT).

item.title: Alcohol and HIT: why it's so hard, and what I do at events
item.description: It blocks DAO, and some drinks are high in histamine. A double hit — and what I order so I don't have to explain a thing.

item.title: How I talk to family and friends about HIT without seeming difficult
item.description: Family meals, the classic "one bite won't hurt you". How I keep it short and solution-focused, without feeling like a burden.

item.title: Exercise and protein with HIT: what works for me
item.description: Movement can be a trigger, and with protein the source matters. Here's what works for me.

item.title: Plant milk with HIT: how I replaced cow's milk
item.description: Which plant-based options work, what to watch out for, and what I reach for instead of cow's milk.

item.title: Breakfast, lunch, dinner: three simple meals I make often
item.description: I'm no chef and I don't like to overcomplicate things. Three simple, fresh, gentle meals I come back to again and again.

item.title: Bad days: what I do when histamine throws off my whole day
item.description: Not every day is the same. How I bring my "bucket" back down and take back control on the hard days.

item.title: Sleep, stress and histamine: the triggers that aren't food
item.description: I have bad days even when I eat "perfectly". About the triggers that have nothing to do with the plate.

item.title: DAO and other supplements: what I tried and what I learned
item.description: The enzyme that breaks down histamine, how it's taken and what blocks it. Approached with care — never without a doctor.

item.title: How I read a restaurant menu when I have HIT
item.description: Eating out doesn't have to be a nightmare. Here's what I ask, what I look for and what I order to stay relaxed.

item.title: Constipation and histamine: the awkward subject no one talks about
item.description: When digestion stalls, histamine builds up from the inside. A key piece almost no one discusses.

item.title: The menstrual cycle and histamine: why some days of the month are harder
item.description: Same foods, same routine, but stronger reactions on certain days. The link between hormones and histamine.

item.title: How I read a label in the supermarket in 10 seconds
item.description: A quick system: a short list, the usual triggers, "fresh vs long shelf life". Relaxed shopping.

item.title: HIT in a life on the move: holidays, traffic and unpredictable days
item.description: Traffic, holidays, unpredictable days — what I always carry so nothing catches me off guard.

item.title: My mistakes in the first month (so you don't make them too)
item.description: I made just about every mistake there was at the start. Here they are, so you can skip them.

item.title: The histamine bucket, explained simply
item.description: The most important concept in HIT. Why a food sits well with you one day and badly the next.

item.title: What to do when you find out you have HIT: frustration, despair and a fresh start
item.description: Relief that it finally has a name, panic at the first list of "forbidden" foods. How I moved forward.

item.title: My safe pantry: what I always keep at home
item.description: Simple things I tolerate, that I can quickly turn into a safe meal — always just 15 minutes away.

item.title: What "wired and tired" really means, and why it isn't (always) anxiety
item.description: Exhausted, but with your brain switched on. Why it can be histamine in the brain, not anxiety — and why that matters.

item.title: The symptoms that took me from one doctor to another, for months
item.description: Itching, migraines, fatigue — and the feeling that no one believed me. What I wish I'd known.

item.title: Why I created MyHistamate
item.description: It didn't start from a business idea, but from a need I was living every single day.
